title: AI-Driven Resume Quality & ATS Analysis System
description: Built an end-to-end AI hiring analysis system that ingested resumes, LinkedIn exports, GitHub metadata, and interview transcripts into a structured PostgreSQL knowledge graph, then scored candidate-to-job alignment using a custom ATS engine powered by sentence-transformers and scikit-learn. Orchestrated a multi-agent LangGraph pipeline across OpenAI, Anthropic, and Ollama models to generate explainable skill gap analyses and targeted resume improvements, with validation logic that strictly grounded all outputs in existing candidate evidence to prevent hallucinated credentials. Delivered the full system through an interactive Textual terminal UI, with reproducible ETL pipelines and documented prompt engineering practices supporting auditable, evidence-backed hiring decisions.

title: California Real Estate Price Prediction
description: Built a machine learning web application that predicts single-family residence prices across California using ensemble models trained on historical real estate data. Implemented and compared XGBoost and LightGBM regressors with scikit-learn preprocessing pipelines, deploying the final model through an interactive Streamlit interface that accepts user-defined property features and returns price estimates in real time. The application incorporates Plotly visualizations and a searchable address interface to provide interpretable, location-aware predictions for prospective homebuyers and researchers.

title: eCommerce Session Review Analysis - Predicting Online Shopper Purchase Intent
description: Built classification models using Random Forest and XGBoost on 12,330 e-commerce browsing sessions to predict purchase intent, applying threshold tuning to optimize recall and reduce missed conversions, achieving ~90% accuracy. Applied K-Means clustering to segment shoppers into five behavioral archetypes, uncovering conversion rates ranging from 0.4% for bounce-prone visitors to 24.3% for high-intent consumers. Identified product browsing depth and administrative page engagement as the strongest purchase predictors using Python, scikit-learn, pandas, and seaborn.

title: Dsc207miniproject
description: Built a data science mini-project using Python to collect, process, and visualize structured datasets through API requests and pandas-based data manipulation. Applied statistical analysis and created visualizations with matplotlib and seaborn to extract meaningful patterns and insights from the data. The project demonstrated end-to-end data pipeline skills, from raw data ingestion to polished visual summaries.

title: Sleepercelmanual
description: Built a manual, notebook-based implementation of a sleeper cell detection system using Python, NumPy, and Pandas to process and evaluate network or behavioral data through hand-crafted logic rather than automated pipelines. Leveraged the Requests library to fetch external datasets and applied custom filtering and aggregation techniques to identify dormant or low-activity patterns indicative of sleeper cell behavior. The project served as a foundational prototype for understanding the underlying mechanics before scaling to a fully automated solution.

title: SleeperCel
description: Built a full-stack AI roster building assistant for fantasy football that integrates with the public Sleeper API to surface personalized league, roster, and player availability data through a React + Vite frontend. Leveraged Python with pandas and NumPy in Jupyter notebooks to process player statistics and power roster optimization recommendations. The tool provides fantasy managers with a streamlined interface to evaluate their rosters and identify available players across their Sleeper leagues.

title: DSC 80 Project Notebook
description: Built a data science pipeline using pandas, NumPy, and scikit-learn to clean, transform, and model a structured dataset, applying techniques such as feature engineering, missingness handling, and predictive modeling. Developed interactive visualizations with Plotly and Matplotlib to communicate patterns and model performance. The project demonstrated proficiency in the full data science workflow from exploratory data analysis through machine learning model evaluation.

title: UCSD Research Lab
description: Built a research pipeline for UCSD professor Andre Gray using Jupyter Notebooks and R to process and model experimental data. Applied statistical methods and data visualization techniques to extract actionable insights from research datasets. Delivered reproducible analyses supporting ongoing academic research at the University of California, San Diego.

title: Bike Traffic in Cambridge Interactive Visualization
description: Built an interactive visualization of bike traffic patterns across Cambridge using JavaScript, CSS, and HTML. Mapped station-level ridership data with dynamic filtering controls, allowing users to toggle between time-of-day views and compare traffic volumes across the city's bike network. The project revealed peak commuting corridors and underutilized routes through proportional symbol mapping and real-time data updates.

title: Desktop Tutorial
description: This project served as a hands-on introduction to GitHub Desktop, demonstrating the core Git workflow of editing files, staging changes, and committing to a repository. The exercise involved modifying a README file and using the GitHub Desktop GUI to track and manage version control without command-line tools. It established foundational familiarity with repository management and the commit-push pipeline used in collaborative software development.

title: How Airbnb Tourism was impacted by the San Diego Sunshine
description: A static graph which explores Airbnb bookings and revenue throughout the year in San Diego, California. The graph shows how the number of bookings and revenue changes throughout the year, with the highest peaks in the summer months.

title: Study on the Impact of Estrus on Female Rats Throughout the Day
description: This graph is a static visualization of the body temperature across male and female mice. Used to explore differences in the daily cylce of body temperature while highlighting the impact of female estrus.

title: AllRecipes Rating Predictive Classification Model
description: This machine learning model predicts the ratings of recipes on AllRecipes.com based on features of the recipe. We created a multiclass-classifier using a RandomForest.

title: Study of the Presence of Educational Institutions and Income Throughout California
description: An exploratory data analysis of the presence of educational institutions and income throughout California. The project uses a heatmap to show the distribution of educational institutions and income throughout the state of California.

title: Image Processor
description: Built an image processor that can read in an image, apply a filter to it, and then output the processed image. The image processor can apply filters such as negate, grayscale, and edge detection. This project was built using Python.

title: Asteroid Landfall Simulation
description: Written using pandas in the Jupyter Notebook environment, this project simulates the landfall of asteroids using historical datasets, probabilistic modeling, and bootstrapping.

title: Interactive Visualization of Mice Body Temperature Across Gender
description: An interactive visualization of the body temperature across the gender of mice. The visualization allows users to explore the differences in body temperature while allowing filtering and the altering of scope. Features a tooltip which highlights Mouse ID allowing us to examine indvidual mice along with their estrus cycle( should they be female).
